title: Autoregressive Generation
concept: Autoregressive models generate text by repeatedly predicting the next word, appending it to the input, and predicting again.

exercises.prompt: What is the main idea behind autoregressive text generation?

options.text: Generating text word-by-word in a loop.
Translating an entire book in one second.

exercises.explain: Autoregressive generation is the process of generating sequence data (like text) one element at a time, using previous outputs as inputs.

cards.title: One Word at a Time
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how ChatGPT writes its answers? It doesn't write whole sentences all at once. Instead, it generates text one single word at a time, incredibly fast!

cards.title: What is Autoregressive?
cards.body: An **autoregressive model** is a type of AI that generates text step-by-step. To predict the next word, it looks at the original prompt plus all the new words it has already generated so far. This process repeats until the text is finished.

cards.title: The Keyboard Writer
cards.body: Think of an autoregressive model like a writer typing on a keyboard one character at a time. The writer cannot write the end of the sentence before the beginning. They must read everything they have already written up to that moment to decide which letter to press next.

cards.title: Step-by-Step in Action
cards.body:
If you ask the AI to finish the sentence **"The cat sat on the..."**, the model will first predict **"mat"**. 

In the next step, it feeds **"The cat sat on the mat"** back into itself to predict the next word, which might be a period **"."**. It keeps looping like this!

cards.title: Do They Plan Ahead?
cards.body:
**Myth:** AI models plan out entire paragraphs and generate them instantly in a single giant leap.
**Fact:** Autoregressive models generate text incrementally. They do not "know" the 100th word they will write until they have generated the 99 words before it!

cards.title: Step-by-Step vs. All-at-Once
cards.body: Let's compare how autoregressive generation differs from non-autoregressive (parallel) generation.

left.text: Generates one word at a time in a loop. Each word depends on the ones before it.

right.text: Generates all words at the same time in a single step.

exercises.prompt: How does an autoregressive model decide what word to generate next?

options.text: It guesses all words at the exact same time.
It generates text from right to left only.
It predicts the next word based on all previously generated words.
It searches the internet for a pre-written paragraph.

exercises.explain: Autoregressive models can only generate the next word by looking back at the prompt and all the words they have generated up to that point.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An autoregressive model must generate word #2 before it can generate word #3.
exercises.explain: Autoregressive models write sequentially, meaning they must generate the first part of a sentence before they can generate the end.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the autoregressive loop cycle.
exercises.sentence: In autoregressive generation, each new word is added back to the input to predict the next one.
exercises.explain: Autoregressive generation works in a loop: the model's new outputs are fed back into its input so it can predict what comes next.

exercises.prompt: Which of these sequences represents true autoregressive generation?

options.text: Input: "The sky is" -> Output: "blue" -> Next Input: "The sky is green"
Input: "The" -> Output: "sky is blue" -> Next Input: "The"

exercises.explain: Response B is the correct example of autoregressive behavior because it shows the output word 'sky' being fed back into the next prediction cycle. The others generate all at once or change the past.

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI: "Write a poem about space." How does an autoregressive AI generate this poem?

options.text: It generates the entire paragraph in a single parallel step.
It predicts one word, adds it to the prompt, and uses this new combination to predict the next word.
It only predicts the very first and last words of a sentence.
It translates words into images before generating text.

exercises.explain: Autoregressive models predict text one step at a time, where each new word or token they create is immediately fed back into their input memory to help decide the next word. This creates a loop that continues until the generation is complete.
